item.name: Transfer a membership to a new homeowner (house-sale flip)

request.name: Transfer a membership to a new homeowner (house-sale flip)

description.content: Flips the membership's `customer_id` to the new owner. Status, dates, visit counters, and the payment ledger all stay attached. Equipment is property-scoped and does NOT migrate — the new owner inherits the property and its attached units automatically. Emits the `membership.transferred` webhook. Cancelled or expired memberships cannot be transferred — re-enroll the new owner instead.

response.name: OK — transfer applied. Body returns the full updated membership with the new `customer_id`. Idempotent: a transfer to the same `customer_id` returns 200 without re-emitting the webhook.

Outbound HTTP webhook deliveries from Run a Call to subscriber URLs.

**Authentication.** Every POST carries `Run-A-Call-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hex>`.
The signed string is `${ts}.${raw_body}`. Verify with HMAC-SHA256 against the
subscription secret. Reject if `Math.abs(now - t) > 5 minutes` to defeat
replay attacks.

**Deduplication.** Each delivery's `event.id` (header
`Run-A-Call-Event-Id`, also in the JSON body) is stable across retries
of the same logical event. Store the last N event ids you've processed
and dedupe before applying business logic.

**Retry policy.** Subscriber returns 2xx within 10 seconds → success.
Anything else (non-2xx, timeout, network error) is retried up to 3 total
attempts with default Inngest backoff. After 10 consecutive terminal
failures, the subscription is auto-paused and the org owner is emailed.
Resume from /settings/integrations/webhooks/{id}.

**Concurrency.** Run a Call caps fan-out at 100 matching subscriptions
per single internal event and concurrent deliveries at 50 in-flight per
function — beyond that, the system bails rather than fan out unbounded.

Returns every appointment of the given job (single round-trip, not paginated — a job's appointment count is bounded). For multi-day installs, ordered by `sequence` ascending. For service calls, returns exactly one appointment.

> A bearer-authenticated REST API for pushing data into and pulling data out of **[Run a Call](https://runacall.com)** — the field service platform built for small-to-mid HVAC companies. Migrate from your old CRM, sync with accounting tools, fire Zapier / Make / n8n flows on real-time events, or wire your own scripts. Same surface internally — no second-class endpoints. ## ⚡ Quick start Three lines to your first request. Replace the token with one from **[Settings → Integrations → API](https://app.runacall.com/settings/integrations/api)** in your dashboard. ```bash curl https://api.runacall.com/v1/customers \ -H "Authorization: Bearer rkc_live_..." ``` That's it. Bearer header, JSON in, JSON out. --- ## 🔑 Authentication Every request needs an `Authorization: Bearer rkc_live_...` header. | Scope | Grants | |---|---| | `read` | All `GET` endpoints | | `write` | `POST` and `PATCH` on customers / properties / equipment / jobs / memberships | Tokens are **SHA-256 hashed at rest**. The raw value is shown to the org owner exactly once at creation — we cannot retrieve it later. Rotate at any time from the same settings page; the old token works for a 24-hour grace window so deploys don't break. --- ## 🛡️ Tenant isolation Every request is scoped to the organization that owns the bearer token. - Cross-org reads return **404 (not 403)** to mask existence - No `admin` scope, no DELETE endpoints, no destructive verbs - Soft-delete (`is_deleted = true`) is a UI-only operation - RLS + service-role org-filter run independently — defense in depth --- ## 🚦 Rate limits | Layer | Limit | Window | |---|---|---| | Per key — sustained | 60 req | 1 min rolling | | Per key — burst | 120 req | 10 s rolling | | Per org — all keys combined | 300 req | 1 min rolling | | Concurrent in-flight per key | 5 | — | | Per-IP — unauthed paths | 10 req | 1 min rolling | Exceeding any layer returns **`429 Too Many Requests`** with a `Retry-After` header. Founders can raise a known integrator's limit via per-key override — email **[admin@runacall.com](mailto:admin@runacall.com)**. --- ## 🔁 Idempotency — upsert by `external_id` Every writable resource (customers, properties, equipment, jobs, memberships) accepts `external_id` + `external_source`. POSTing the same pair twice **upserts the existing row** — returns 200 with the same UUID instead of 201 with a duplicate. ```bash # First call — creates curl -X POST https://api.runacall.com/v1/customers \ -H "Authorization: Bearer rkc_live_..." \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"external_id":"crm-acme-001","external_source":"servicetitan","first_name":"Jenny","last_name":"Rosen"}' # → 201 Created, id: 497f6eca-6276-4993-bfeb-53cbbbba6f08 # Same call again — upserts, same id curl -X POST https://api.runacall.com/v1/customers \ -H "Authorization: Bearer rkc_live_..." \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"external_id":"crm-acme-001","external_source":"servicetitan","first_name":"Jenny","last_name":"Rosen"}' # → 200 OK, id: 497f6eca-6276-4993-bfeb-53cbbbba6f08 ``` Safe to retry. Safe to script bulk imports against. Network blip? Re-fire — you'll never end up with duplicates. --- ## 📄 Pagination Cursor-based, never offset. Pass back the `next_cursor` from a response as `?starting_after=` to fetch the next page. ```json { "data": [ /* up to 100 items */ ], "has_more": true, "next_cursor": "MjAyNi0wNS0wN1QwODo1OTowMi4xMjIzNDQrMDA6MDB8MzZhNTlmMDQ" } ``` Default page size 50, hard cap 100. There's no way to ask for "all rows" in a single call — keep your loop honest. --- ## ❌ Errors Every error response is the same envelope: ```json { "error": { "code": "invalid_request", "message": "external_id is required when upserting", "doc_url": "https://docs.runacall.com/#errors-invalid_request", "request_id": "req_a93a7817f5a8485d80204becea" } } ``` Quote **`request_id`** when reaching out for support — it correlates to our audit log. ### `invalid_request` — 400 / 422 Body or query parameter validation failed. The `message` field names the specific issue. Common causes: missing required field, unknown field (`.strict()` rejects extras), `external_id` without `external_source`, FK reference not found in this organization, invalid job status transition. ### `unauthorized` — 401 Missing or invalid bearer token. Check the `Authorization` header format: `Bearer rkc_live_...`. If you rotated the token recently, the old value works for 24 hours — verify you're using the right one. ### `forbidden` — 403 Your token lacks the required scope. `POST` and `PATCH` endpoints require the `write` scope; everything else needs `read`. Issue a new key with the right scopes from the Settings page. ### `not_found` — 404 The resource doesn't exist, OR it belongs to another organization. We mask cross-org existence to prevent enumeration attacks — there's no way to tell from outside which case it is. ### `conflict` — 409 Concurrent upsert race lost. Two POSTs hit the same `(external_id, external_source)` at once; only one won the unique-index race. **Retry the same payload** — your second attempt will hit the existing row via the upsert path and return 200. ### `rate_limited` — 429 One of the rate-limit layers above kicked in. The response includes a `Retry-After` header (seconds). Back off and retry. ### `internal_error` — 500 A bug on our end. Quote the `request_id` when emailing **admin@runacall.com** and we'll investigate. We log every 5xx response with full context. --- ## 🪝 Webhooks Push real-time events to your own endpoints instead of polling. Subscribe a URL via **[Settings → Integrations → Webhooks](https://app.runacall.com/settings/integrations/webhooks)**. **15 canonical events** across customers, jobs, estimates, invoices, payments, voice calls, and memberships. Every delivery is **HMAC-SHA256 signed** over `\${ts}.\${raw_body}` — header: `Run-A-Call-Signature: t=,v1=`. Verify the timestamp is within 5 minutes (replay protection), then verify the signature. Failed deliveries retry 3x with exponential backoff. After **10 consecutive failures** the subscription auto-pauses and we email the org owner — resume with one click. See the **Webhooks** section in the sidebar for the full event catalog + payload shapes. --- ## 📦 What you can build A few patterns the API was designed for: - **One-shot migration from your old CRM** — script a pull from ServiceTitan / Housecall Pro / a spreadsheet → POST against `/customers` + `/properties` + `/equipment` with your own `external_id`. Re-run safely; idempotent. - **Accounting sync** — subscribe to `invoice.paid` + `payment.received` webhooks → push into QuickBooks / Xero / your ERP. - **Zapier / Make / n8n automation** — webhook triggers fan out to Slack, Notion, Google Sheets, anywhere. - **Custom analytics** — `GET /voice-calls/{id}` returns transcripts. No other HVAC platform exposes this. Need an integration we haven't documented yet? Email **[admin@runacall.com](mailto:admin@runacall.com)** with what you're trying to do and we'll wire it. Contact Support: Name: Run a Call Support Email: admin@runacall.com
